2603.26236.title: A Universal Vibe? Finding and Controlling Language-Agnostic Informal Register with SAEs
2603.26236.paper_summary: While multilingual language models successfully transfer factual and syntactic knowledge across languages, it remains unclear whether they process culture-specific pragmatic registers, such as slang, as isolated language-specific memorizations or as unified, abstract concepts. We study this by probing the internal representations of Gemma-2-9B-IT using Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) across three typologically diverse source languages: English, Hebrew, and Russian. To definitively isolate pragmatic register processing from trivial lexical sensitivity, we introduce a novel dataset in which every target term is polysemous, appearing in both literal and informal contexts. We find that while much of the informal-register signal is distributed across language-specific features, a small but highly robust cross-linguistic core consistently emerges. This shared core forms a geometrically coherent ``informal register subspace'' that sharpens in the model's deeper layers. Crucially, these shared representations are not merely correlational: activation steering with these features causally shifts output formality across all source languages and transfers zero-shot to six unseen languages spanning diverse language families and scripts. Together, these results provide the first mechanistic evidence that multilingual LLMs internalize informal register not just as surface-level heuristics, but as a portable, language-agnostic pragmatic abstraction.
2603.26236.paper_summary_zh: 雖然多語言語言模型成功地在不同語言之間轉移事實和語法知識，但仍不清楚它們是否將文化特定的語用註冊（例如俚語）視為孤立的語言特定記憶，還是作為統一的抽象概念。我們通過使用稀疏自編碼器（SAEs）探測 Gemma-2-9B-IT 的內部表示，來研究這一點，涵蓋三種類型學上多樣的源語言：英語、希伯來語和俄語。為了明確將語用註冊處理與簡單的詞彙敏感性隔離，我們引入了一個新數據集，其中每個目標詞都是多義的，出現在字面和非正式的上下文中。我們發現，雖然許多非正式註冊信號分佈在語言特定特徵中，但一個小而高度穩健的跨語言核心始終出現。這個共享核心形成了一個幾何一致的“非正式註冊子空間”，在模型的更深層中變得更加明確。關鍵是，這些共享表示不僅僅是相關性的：這些特徵的激活引導會在所有源語言中因果地改變輸出形式，並在六種未見語言中零樣本轉移，這些語言跨越多樣的語言家族和書寫系統。總體而言，這些結果提供了第一個機制證據，表明多語言 LLM 不僅將非正式註冊內化為表層啟發式，而是作為一種可攜帶的、與語言無關的語用抽象。
2603.26236.author: Uri Z. Kialy et.al.
2603.26236.authors: Uri Z. Kialy, Avi Shtarkberg, Ayal Klein
